subject: FW: Very Special update from Andrea Jaeger----Extremely Time Sensitive
body:
Jeffrey, 


I hope you are well.  I often ask Ghislaine about you and she always
gives a glowing report.  Things in NYC are well. Things with CGI busy as
ever, hard to believe it has been 4 years.  We just hosted our CGI U
meeting at Tulane. We had 600 students from 45 states and 263
universities - it turned out really well, we were all very pleased. Here
is a video clip of it (it takes a little bit of time to load)  
http://newsroom.mtv.com/2008/03/21/cgi-u-meet-some-of-the-countrys-most-
committed-college-students/.  We will have our CGI Int'l meeting in Hong
Kong in early December and prior to it have our CGI NY meeting here in
the city in September. All in all things are going well and we will
never forget those who believed in us from the beginning. One of my
favorite quotes is on the US Navy Seabees Memorial:  "With a skillful
hand and a willing heart, the difficult we will do at once the
impossible, may just take a bit more time!" It certainly has proven true
in CGI land :-) 

 

I called Ghislaine about something I wanted to bring to your attention.
She said I should just forward it to you directly, hope you do not mind.


 

Andrea Yaeger http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andrea_Jaeger reached out to
me and asked for me to make an introduction to you.  I met Andrea 2
years ago at CGI.  You probably would remember her - Andrea (see
articles below) was a professional tennis player at a young age but then
realized her calling was really to help children.  She used her winnings
to start an organization to help children with cancer.  It has become a
haven for children who want to just be children :-) 

Andrea is one of the most positive people I have ever met.  She has a
large donor who is willing to make a substantive match to funds she
raises - so this past year she has been tirelessly out there trying to
raise money - all the while remaining positive, happy, gracious, funny
and kind.  

 

Andrea sent me the email below after doing some research about possible
donors.  I do hope you will be willing to meet her, though be prepared,
if you spend 5 minutes with her you will want to give her everything -
she is just that kind - talk about someone who wears her heart on her
sleeve.  

 

Here is her organization's website: http://www.littlestar.org/

 

Here are some articles about Andrea:

http://www.people.com/people/article/0,26334,1561084,00.html

http://sportsillustrated.cnn.com/2006/writers/richard_deitsch/12/08/medi
a.circus/index.html

 

Let me know what you think - I would be happy to do a virtual intro
between the two of you - or you could reach out to her yourself.  

Thank you very much for considering it Jeffrey. 


Take care of yourself. 

 

Mary 

________________________________

From: AJLittlestar@aol.com [mailto:AJLittlestar@aol.com] 
Sent: Sunday, April 13, 2008 2:30 PM
To: Mary Morrison
Subject: Very Special update from Andrea Jaeger----Extremely Time
Sensitive

 

Mary---

 

After 18 years of fundraising research and children's cancer programs I
believe a gentleman that can help me as an investor or donor is Jeffrey
Epstein. 

During my research I came across an extraordinary individual that has
continuously provided the missing link in people reaching their greatest
potential. Jeffrey Epstein utilizes his vision and financial and
business expertise to invest in people. The results are transformational
for programs on a global scale that would have been unrealized without
Jeffrey's involvement. 

 

Our interests compliment each other and our combined efforts will create
a unique opportunity to better the world. 

 

I need to raise a $40 million dollar endowment, I have a match campaign
going on and we do have a property that if someone prefers to invest
rather than donate that opportunity is also available and it would get
us half way to the endowment. 

 

I am attaching our recent newsletter. An Oprah feature is running on us
next week some time. 

 

President Clinton, through a spokesperson said this about Jeffrey
Epstein, "Jeffrey is both a highly successful financier and a committed
philanthropist with a keen sense of global markets and an in-depth
knowledge of twenty-first century science. I especially appreciated his
insights and generosity during the recent trip to Africa to work on
democratization, empowering the poor, citizen service and combating
HIV/AIDS." 

  

Mary, I need to get a meeting with him, I really think he could help us.
Can you help me?

 

Please let me know what you think.

Thanks!

Love and blessings,

Andrea Jaeger

cell 970-948-6056

AJLittlestar@aol.com





________________________________

It's Tax Time! Get tips, forms and advice on AOL Money & Finance
<http://money.aol.com/tax?NCID=aolcmp00300000002850> .

Jeffrey,


I hope you are well.  I often ask Ghislaine about you and she always gives a glowing report.  Things in NYC are well. Things with CGI busy as ever, hard to believe it has been 4 years.  We just hosted our CGI U meeting at Tulane. We had 600 students from 45 states and 263 universities – it turned out really well, we were all very pleased. Here is a video clip of it (it takes a little bit of time to load)  http://newsroom.mtv.com/2008/03/21/cgi-u-meet-some-of-the-countrys-most-committed-college-students/.  We will have our CGI Int’l meeting in Hong Kong in early December and prior to it have our CGI NY meeting here in the city in September. All in all things are going well and we will never forget those who believed in us from the beginning. One of my favorite quotes is on the US Navy Seabees Memorial:  “With a skillful hand and a willing heart, the difficult we will do at once the impossible, may just take a bit more time!” It certainly has proven true in CGI land J

 

I called Ghislaine about something I wanted to bring to your attention.  She said I should just forward it to you directly, hope you do not mind.  

 

Andrea Yaeger http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andrea_Jaeger reached out to me and asked for me to make an introduction to you.  I met Andrea 2 years ago at CGI.  You probably would remember her - Andrea (see articles below) was a professional tennis player at a young age but then realized her calling was really to help children.  She used her winnings to start an organization to help children with cancer.  It has become a haven for children who want to just be children J

Andrea is one of the most positive people I have ever met.  She has a large donor who is willing to make a substantive match to funds she raises - so this past year she has been tirelessly out there trying to raise money – all the while remaining positive, happy, gracious, funny and kind. 

 

Andrea sent me the email below after doing some research about possible donors.  I do hope you will be willing to meet her, though be prepared, if you spend 5 minutes with her you will want to give her everything – she is just that kind – talk about someone who wears her heart on her sleeve. 

 

Here is her organization’s website: http://www.littlestar.org/

 

Here are some articles about Andrea:

http://www.people.com/people/article/0,26334,1561084,00.html

http://sportsillustrated.cnn.com/2006/writers/richard_deitsch/12/08/media.circus/index.html

 

Let me know what you think – I would be happy to do a virtual intro between the two of you – or you could reach out to her yourself. 

Thank you very much for considering it Jeffrey.


Take care of yourself.

 

Mary

From: AJLittlestar@aol.com [mailto:AJLittlestar@aol.com]
Sent: Sunday, April 13, 2008 2:30 PM
To: Mary Morrison
Subject: Very Special update from Andrea Jaeger----Extremely Time Sensitive

 

Mary---

 

After 18 years of fundraising research and children's cancer programs I believe a gentleman that can help me as an investor or donor is Jeffrey Epstein.

During my research I came across an extraordinary individual that has continuously provided the missing link in people reaching their greatest potential. Jeffrey Epstein utilizes his vision and financial and business expertise to invest in people. The results are transformational for programs on a global scale that would have been unrealized without Jeffrey's involvement. 

 

Our interests compliment each other and our combined efforts will create a unique opportunity to better the world. 

 

I need to raise a $40 million dollar endowment, I have a match campaign going on and we do have a property that if someone prefers to invest rather than donate that opportunity is also available and it would get us half way to the endowment. 

 

I am attaching our recent newsletter. An Oprah feature is running on us next week some time.

 

President Clinton, through a spokesperson said this about Jeffrey Epstein, "Jeffrey is both a highly successful financier and a committed philanthropist with a keen sense of global markets and an in-depth knowledge of twenty-first century science. I especially appreciated his insights and generosity during the recent trip to Africa to work on democratization, empowering the poor, citizen service and combating HIV/AIDS."

  

Mary, I need to get a meeting with him, I really think he could help us.  Can you help me?

 

Please let me know what you think.

Thanks!

Love and blessings,

Andrea Jaeger
